notes: Sub 8: T-scaling (T=4.073) AND per-subject mean-residual offset re-enabled. Hypothesis: sub 2 showed offset hurts when raw predictions are wildly overconfident (mean residuals get dominated by sigmoid saturation). With T-scaling softening predictions to well-calibrated probabilities (sub 5 NLL=-0.65), the offset may now correctly capture per-subject systematic bias from the K=5 labeled examples. If sub 8 beats sub 5, offset+calibration is a valid stack; if it ties or loses, the K=5 signal is too sparse to add value beyond global calibration.
